content:
# Revelation Chapter 10 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (a), 4th century CE, pages 284-285 | **Source Image:** REV_010_page_001.jpg, REV_010_page_002.jpg

1. And I saw another mighty angel coming down out of heaven, clothed with a cloud, and the rainbow was upon his head, and his face was like the sun, and his feet like pillars of fire.

2. And he had in his hand a little scroll that had been opened. And he set his right foot upon the sea and his left upon the land,

3. and he cried out with a loud voice, as a lion roars. And when he cried out, the seven thunders uttered their own voices.

4. And when the seven thunders spoke, I was about to write; and I heard a voice from heaven saying, "Seal up the things which the seven thunders spoke, and do not write them."

5. And the angel whom I saw standing upon the sea and upon the land raised his right hand to heaven,

6. and swore by him who lives forever and ever, who created the heaven and the things in it, and the earth and the things in it, and the sea and the things in it, that there shall be delay no longer,

7. but in the days of the voice of the seventh angel, when he is about to sound his trumpet, then the mystery of God is completed, as he proclaimed the good news to his servants the prophets.

8. And the voice which I heard from heaven was again speaking with me and saying, "Go, take the scroll which is opened in the hand of the angel who stands upon the sea and upon the land."

9. And I went to the angel, telling him to give me the little scroll. And he says to me, "Take it and eat it up, and it shall make your stomach bitter, but in your mouth it shall be sweet as honey."

10. And I took the little scroll out of the hand of the angel and ate it up, and it was in my mouth sweet as honey; and when I had eaten it, my stomach was made bitter.

11. And they say to me, "You must prophesy again concerning many peoples and nations and tongues and kings."

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

- **10:1 — "clothed with a cloud"**: Sinaiticus reads ΠΕΡΙΒΕΒΛΗΜΕΝΟΝ ΝΕΦΕΛΗΝ. The perfect passive participle conveys the angel as wrapped or enveloped in cloud, rendered "clothed with" for formal precision.
- **10:2 — "little scroll" (biblaridion)**: Sinaiticus uses the diminutive ΒΙΒΛΑΡΙΔΙΟΝ (vv. 2, 9, 10), distinguished from the larger ΒΙΒΛΙΟΝ of chapter 5. Translated consistently as "little scroll" to preserve the diminutive form.
- **10:4 — "Seal up"**: Greek ΣΦΡΑΓΙΣΟΝ (aorist imperative) — a decisive command to conceal, not merely to stop writing. The sealing implies divine restriction on disclosure.
- **10:6 — "delay no longer"**: Sinaiticus reads ΧΡΟΝΟΣ ΟΥΚΕΤΙ ΕΣΤΑΙ. Rendered "delay no longer" rather than "time no longer" — chronos here refers to an interval of waiting, not to time as a metaphysical concept.
- **10:7 — "the mystery of God is completed"**: ΕΤΕΛΕΣΘΗ (aorist passive), rendered as present "is completed" following the proleptic/prophetic aorist usage — the future event spoken of as already accomplished.
- **10:7 — "proclaimed the good news"**: ΕΥΗΓΓΕΛΙΣΕΝ — the verb euangelizō is rendered "proclaimed the good news" rather than simply "declared," preserving the gospel-proclamation overtone.
- **10:9 — "he says to me"**: Historical present ΛΕΓΕΙ retained as "says" (not "said") to preserve the Greek narrative vividness.
- **10:11 — "they say to me"**: The plural ΛΕΓΟΥΣΙΝ with indefinite subject. Sinaiticus has the plural; the identity of the speakers is left unspecified, as in the Greek.

— LOGOS
